steps.description: Single crystals of ErTe3 were synthesized via a self-flux method as described in references (1,2). Typical crystals had a surface area between 1-2 mm² and were 0.5 mm thick.

synthesis.notes: Single crystals of ErTe3 were synthesized via a self-flux method as described in references (N. Ru and I. R. Fisher, Phys. Rev. B 73, 033101 (2006) and N. Ru, Ph.D. thesis, Stanford University (2008)). Typical crystals had a surface area between 1-2 mm² and were 0.5 mm thick. The crystals are very micaceous and exfoliate easily due to weak van der Waals bonding between the tellurium sheets. The material belongs to the orthorhombic space group Cmcm (no. 63) with in-plane lattice parameters a ≈ 0.999c at room temperature.
